evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Ability to work independently in fast-paced environments
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Mixed: 'fast-paced environments' is a velocity signal, while data access controls, Row Level Security, and GDPR-compliant reporting are rigour signals, giving rough parity.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Property/real estate operational reporting with a GDPR compliance mention but no financial-reporting or audit language, so moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Creating dashboards replacing legacy spreadsheets with single-source-of-truth equivalents
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Migrating from legacy spreadsheets to a structured BI layer using an existing enterprise system (Yardi) suggests a growing, still-developing reporting function.
evidence.jd_authorship: Building data models using Yardi and Power Query transformations
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (Power BI, Yardi, Power Query, DAX) and specific metrics (void analysis, case ageing, yield and occupancy) make the day-to-day work very clear.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: design, build and maintain Power BI dashboards across operations, leasing, asset
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role serves internal reporting/dashboard consumers across multiple business functions via a self-service BI layer, placing it in internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: Supporting stakeholder engagement and dashboard adoption
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Verbs are largely execution-oriented (design, build, maintain, support) delivering scoped dashboard work rather than setting broader data strategy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: No testing or data quality signal.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: No testing, data contracts, observability, or data quality language appears anywhere in the JD.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Implementing data access controls and Row Level Security
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: access controls and GDPR-compliant reporting are mentioned but framed as standard operational safeguards rather than a dominant risk/trust narrative.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Creating dashboards replacing legacy spreadsheets with single-source-of-truth equivalents
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The dominant work is replacing/improving existing legacy spreadsheet reporting rather than building wholly new infrastructure.
